description: Static-lint a Python file for DALI dynamic-mode (ndd) anti-patterns and pipeline-mode leftovers (read-only)
rules: read-only — never imports DALI, never runs the target file
scans for pipeline-mode leftovers: @pipeline_def, pipe.build(), pipe.run(), fn.* and ndd.fn.* operators
scans for dynamic-mode mistakes: device="mixed", lowercase reader classes (ndd.readers.file), batch[i] indexing, scattered .evaluate() calls, random ops missing batch_size
usage: Set SOURCE_FILE (absolute path to the .py file to review). Output: findings.json (a list of {rule, line, message}).
limitation: Static lint only — it flags documented anti-patterns by source-text/AST analysis; it does not import DALI, execute the code, or verify runtime behavior. Dynamic mode itself can have slightly worse performance than pipeline mode (NVIDIA: for maximum throughput prefer pipeline mode). GPU decode / GPU operators need a CUDA-capable DALI build at actual run time; this perk does not.
